commands.review_summary:
Adds a bundled `openai-compatible-embeddings` plugin, generated plugin docs/inventory metadata, labeler metadata, lockfile importer entry, and HTTP-fixture tests for explicit OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` providers.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a bug report. The changed behavior is source-reviewable and fixture-tested, but there is no failing current-main reproduction path to establish.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to review the protected stacked provider/auth boundary and require real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.

target.title: feat(plugins): add OpenAI-compatible embedding provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a bundled `openai-compatible-embeddings` plugin that registers the generic `openai-compatible` embedding provider from #84947.
- Keep it explicit-only: it requires a configured `remote.baseUrl` and `model`, skips warmup/preload, and does not inherit global OpenAI chat credentials.
- Cover OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` HTTP fixture behavior, docs, plugin inventory, labeler metadata, and workspace lockfile importer.

Stacked on #84991, which adapts memory-core to consume generic embedding providers.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoints can be exposed through the generic `embeddingProviders` contract and then used by memory-core via the stack below this PR.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout with HTTP fixture servers; no live third-party endpoint was called.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/src/embedding-provider.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/src/embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/embedding-provider.contract.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/package-manifest.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/extension-runtime-dependencies.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/inventory/bundled-capability-metadata.test.ts test/plugin-npm-package-manifest.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-openai-compatible-embeddings.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-openai-compatible-embeddings.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/index.ts extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/src/embedding-provider.ts extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/src/embedding-provider.test.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`; `node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/memory-lancedb.md docs/reference/memory-config.md docs/plugins/plugin-inventory.md docs/plugins/reference.md docs/plugins/reference/openai-compatible-embeddings.md`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: fixture tests passed for OpenAI-compatible request shape, no warmup during create, no implicit auth header, explicit bearer auth, structured text handling, parser failures, plugin-entry registration, and sanitized cache metadata.
Observed result after fix: the plugin registers `openai-compatible` through `api.registerEmbeddingProvider`, its manifest declares `contracts.embeddingProviders`, and memory docs point explicit custom endpoints to `provider: "openai-compatible"`.
What was not tested: live provider-specific compatibility with an external OpenAI-compatible service; that should be tested manually with a real endpoint/API key before declaring broad provider coverage.

Note: `pnpm exec oxfmt` tried to reconcile dependencies and hit the repo's current minimum-release-age gate for an unrelated fresh `@anthropic-ai/sdk` release. Formatting was verified through the checked-in local `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt` binary instead, and the lockfile importer was refreshed with `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts`.
target.author: mbelinky
